title: SQRT — Random-frequency SING zap (Robotron)
tldr: SING engine with the frequency seeded from the LFSR random source — every fire produces a different pitch. The "zap" with unpredictable timbre.
how: Before invoking SING, SQRT reads from the `RANDOM` cell (the LFSR's LO byte, conveniently a free random source) and uses it as the initial frequency. So each fire of `$34` gives a different note — useful for variety in what would otherwise be a repetitive enemy-zap sound.
watch: Fire SQRT several times in a row and listen to pitch variations.
Code panel — check `RANDOM` ($06 on Robotron) cell at fire-time.
see: **LITE** — the LFSR that SQRT borrows from.
